info.title: Kinetic Task REST API
info.description:
## Kinetic Task API v2


Kinetic Task is the workflow component of the Kinetic Platform. Kinetic Task is responsible for processing workflows (`trees` and `routines`) built within the Kinetic Platform. Each time a workflow executes, a `run` is created and can be referenced to track the state of a given workflow execution.


## Authentication
Starting in Platform v5, calls to this API are typically proxied through core using the `https://space.kinetic-platform-location.com/app/components/task` base route. During setup, calls can also be made directly to the Kinetic Task application by authenticating with the Configurator User using the `https://my-web-server.com/kinetic-task` base route.


Most of the API requests require user authentication. If an authenticated session has already been established, then the user identity held in the session will be used. Otherwise a Basic Authentication header can be included with the request, supplying the token associated to the username and password.


Kinetic Task currently only supports HTTP Basic authentication for REST API access.


### HTTP Basic Authentication

* Combine the username and password into a string separated by a colon (:)

    ```
    username:password
    ```

* Encode the string using Base64

* Add the encoded string to your request using the Authorization header, prefixed with `Basic `

    ```
    Authorization: Basic dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=
    ```

## Response Format

All responses will be returned using the JSON format.

externalDocs.description: More information is available on Kinetic Community

serverUrl.description: Fully qualified URL to a space within the Kinetic Platform

get.description: Returns list of Access Keys

post.description: Creates a new access key

requestBody.description: The content for the access key properties

get.description: Retrieves the Access Key identified by the {id} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Access Key identified by the {id} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the access key properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Access Key identified by the {id} parameter.

get.description: Returns list of Categories

post.description: Creates a new category

requestBody.description: The content for the category properties

get.description: Retrieves the Category identified by the {name} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Category identified by the {name} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the category properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Category identified by the {name} parameter.

post.description: Adds the Handler to the Category specified by the {name} parameter.

requestBody.description: The content for the handler properties

delete.description: Removes the Handler specified by the {definitionId} parameter from the Category specified by the {name} parameter.

post.description: Adds the Routine to the Category specified by the {name} parameter.

requestBody.description: The content for the routine properties

delete.description: Removes the Routine specified by the {definitionId} parameter from the Category specified by the {name} parameter.

post.description: Adds the Policy Rule to the Category specified by the {name} parameter.

requestBody.description: The content for the policy rule properties

delete.description: Removes the Policy Rule specified by the {policyRuleType} and {policyRuleName} parameters from the Category specified by the {name} parameter.

get.description: Returns information about the authentication configuration.

requestBody.description: The content for the authentication properties

get.description: Returns information about the database configuration.

requestBody.description: The content for the database properties

get.description: Returns information about the engine configuration.

requestBody.description: The content for the engine properties

get.description: Returns information about the identity store configuration.

requestBody.description: The content for the identity store properties

get.description: Returns information about the session configuration.

requestBody.description: The content for the session properties

post.description: Re-encrypt all sensitive values with a new encryption key.

get.description: Returns the system policy rule.

put.description: Updates the system policy rule configuration.

requestBody.description: The content for the system policy rule properties

get.description: Returns information about the task engine for the connected web server.

post.description: Starts or stops the task engine.

requestBody.description: The content for the engine properties

get.description: Returns information about the system.

Returns information about all errors that match the search parameters.

### Examples

Below are a few examples that demonstrate some common queries.  Each example illustrates
the parameters that need to be sent as request parameters.

#### Active errors for a handler

  ```json
  {
    "handlerId": "utility_json_to_xml_v1",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### All errors for a run

  ```json
  {
    "runId": "555"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors for a source

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors for a source id

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request",
    "sourceId": "cefa0736-724d-11e7-8783-11d1da2061fc",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors for a tree

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request",
    "group": "services > laptop-order",
    "tree": "Submitted",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors for a global routine

  ```json
  {
    "source": "-",
    "group": "-",
    "tree": "Calculate SLA",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors for a node in a tree

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request",
    "group": "services > laptop-order",
    "tree": "Submitted",
    "nodeId": "system_join_v1_11",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors for a date range

(timeline defaults to *createdAt*)

  ```json
  {
    "start": "2017-01-01",
    "end": "2017-02-01",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors after a certain date

(timeline defaults to *createdAt*)

  ```json
  {
    "start": "2017-01-01",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```

#### Active errors before a certain date

(timeline defaults to *createdAt*)

  ```json
  {
    "end": "2017-01-01",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```
Retrieves the Error identified by the {id} parameter.

delete.description: Deletes the Error identified by the {id} parameter.

post.description: Resolves a list of errors with the same action and resolution notes.

requestBody.description: The content for the error properties

get.description: Returns list of Groups

post.description: Creates a new group

requestBody.description: The content for the group properties

get.description: Retrieves the Group identified by the {name} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Group identified by the {name} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the group properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Group identified by the {name} parameter.

post.description: Add a User to the Group specified by the {name} parameter.

requestBody.description: The loginId of the user to add to the group

delete.description: Removes a User specified by the {loginId} parameter from the Group specified by the {name} parameter.

get.description: Returns list of Handlers

post.description:
Uploads a handler zip package from either a local file or a URL.

When uploading a local file, the action must use multipart/form-data as the content type.

When uploading from a URL, the action must use application/json as the content type.

requestBody.description: The content for the handler properties

get.description: Retrieves the Handler identified by the {definitionId} parameter.

requestBody.description:
The content for the handler properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Handler identified by the {definitionId} parameter.

get.description: Show duration of last 100 task executions for this handler.
Lists handlers that are used in trees, but missing from the system.
List of available actions to take for each type of unhandled error.
List of registered source adapters with configurable properties.
Displays version information about Task and API.
List of all notices.
Returns information about all policy rules.
Returns information about all policy rules for the particular {type} variable.

post.description: Creates a new Policy Rule for the particular {type} parameter.

requestBody.description: The content for the policy rule properties

get.description: Retrieves the Handler identified by the {type} and {name} parameters.

put.description:
Updates the policy rule specified by the {type} and {name} parameters with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the policy rule properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Policy Rule specified by the {type} and {name} parameters.

get.description: Search Runs

post.description: Creates a new run of the specified tree.

requestBody.description: The content for the run properties

get.description: Retrieves the Run identified by the {id} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Run identified by the {id} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the run properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Run identified by the {id} parameter.

get.description: Returns information about all tasks for a specific run.
Retrieves the Task identified by the {taskId} parameter for a Run identified by the {id} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Task identified by the {taskId} parameter for a Run identified by the {id} parameter.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the task properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

post.description:
Runs the node identified by the {nodeId} body parameter for a Run identified by the {id} parameter.


This will create a root trigger for the node, which will be processed by the Task Engine to run the task handler for this node.

requestBody.description: The content for the root trigger properties

post.description: Completes the deferred task identified by the {token} parameter.

requestBody.description: The content to complete the task

put.description: Updates the deferred task identified by the {token} parameter.

requestBody.description: The content to update the task

get.description: Returns list of Sources

post.description: Creates a new source

requestBody.description: The content for the source properties

get.description: Retrieves the Source identified by the {name} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Source identified by the {name} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the source properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Source identified by the {name} parameter.

post.description: Adds the Policy Rule to the Source specified by the {name} parameter.

requestBody.description:
The content for the source properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Removes the Policy Rule specified by the {policyRuleType} and {policyRuleName} parameters from the Source specified by the {sourceName} parameter.

post.description: Validates a source by using the configured source properties and connecting to the source.

get.description:
Returns information about all system errors that match the search parameters.

### Examples
Below are a few examples that demonstrate some common queries.  Each example illustrates
the parameters that need to be sent as request parameters.
**Active system errors for a date range** (timeline defaults to *createdAt*)
  ```json
  {
    "start": "2017-01-01",
    "end": "2017-02-01",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```
**Active system errors after a certain date** (timeline defaults to *createdAt*)
  ```json
  {
    "start": "2017-01-01",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```
**Active system errors before a certain date** (timeline defaults to *createdAt*)
  ```json
  {
    "end": "2017-01-01",
    "status": "Active"
  }
  ```
Retrieves the System Error identified by the {id} parameter.

delete.description: Deletes the System Error identified by the {id} parameter.

post.description: Resolves a list of system errors with the same action and resolution notes.

requestBody.description: The content for the system error properties

get.description: Returns information about all triggers that match the search parameters.

post.description: Create a new tree from form fields, or from importing a tree file. If importing a tree file as an attachment, must use multipart/form-data content-type, and use the content form field to upload the tree file. Otherwise if importing a tree file from a URL, must use application/json content-type, and use the contentUrl body content property to specify the path the tree file. Otherwise if creating a tree from form fields, must use application/json content-type, and use the sourceName, sourceGroup, and name body content properties to specify the tree values.

requestBody.description: The content for the tree properties

get.description: Lists routines that are used in trees, but missing from the system.
Retrieves the Tree identified by the {title} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Tree identified by the {title} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

When updating the tree content, either the `treeXml` parameter or the `treeJson`
parameter may be included, but not both.

The `treeXml` parameter contains the tree content formatted as an XML document,
and the `treeJson` parameter contains the tree content formatted as JSON content.

requestBody.description:
The content for the tree properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the Tree identified by the {title} parameter.

post.description:
Clones the Tree specified by the {title} parameter.

The `definitionId` property is recommended when cloning a routine, otherwise
a timestamp will be added to the definitionId of the cloned routine.

requestBody.description: The properties for the cloned tree.

get.description: Exports the Tree identified by the {title} parameter.

post.description: Restores the deleted Tree specified by the {title} parameter.

get.description: Lists the run and error counts for the past 30 days for the tree identified by the {title} parameter.
Lists the trees where the routine identified by the {definitionId} parameter is used.

Returns information about all triggers that match the search parameters.

## Examples

Below are a few examples that demonstrate some common queries.  Each example illustrates
the parameters that need to be sent as request parameters.

### All triggers for a run

  ```json
  {
    "runId": "555"
  }
  ```

### All triggers for a branch

  ```json
  {
    "branchId": "555"
  }
  ```

### All triggers for a source

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request"
  }
  ```

### All triggers for a source id

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request",
    "sourceId": "cefa0736-724d-11e7-8783-11d1da2061fc"
  }
  ```

### All triggers for a tree

  ```json
  {
    "source": "Kinetic Request",
    "group": "services > laptop-order",
    "tree": "Submitted"
  }
  ```

### All triggers for a global routine

  ```json
  {
    "source": "-",
    "group": "-",
    "tree": "Calculate SLA"
  }
  ```

### All triggers for a date range

(timeline defaults to *createdAt*)

  ```json
  {
    "start": "2017-01-01",
    "end": "2017-02-01"
  }
  ```

### All triggers after a certain date

(timeline defaults to *createdAt*)

  ```json
  {
    "start": "2017-01-01"
  }
  ```

### All triggers before a certain date

(timeline defaults to *createdAt*)

  ```json
  {
    "end": "2017-01-01"
  }
  ```
Retrieves the Trigger identified by the {id} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the Trigger identified by the {id} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the user properties to update

The `status` property is currently only supported, and must be set to `Unpaused`.

delete.description: Deletes the Trigger identified by the {id} parameter.

get.description:
Returns information about backlogged triggers.

Backlogged triggers are due to be processed, meaning trigger status is New or Unpaused, and the scheduled time is earlier than the current time.

Returns information about paused triggers.

Triggers become paused either manually, or when triggers get created for a paused handler.

Returns information about scheduled triggers.

Scheduled triggers are created in the system, but scheduled to run at a later time.
Returns list of Users

post.description: Creates a new user

requestBody.description: The content for the user properties

get.description: Retrieves the User identified by the {username} parameter.

put.description:
Updates the User identified by the {username} parameter with the property values sent in the request body.

**NOTE** All properties in the request body are optional, and only the properties supplied will be updated.

requestBody.description:
The content for the user properties to update

**Only** the properties supplied will be updated.

delete.description: Deletes the User identified by the {username} parameter.

value.name: Neat Routine
value.notes: Runs when called from another tree or routine.
value.title: - :: - :: Neat Routine

outputs.name: Adjusted Cost
outputs.description: The total adjusted cost of the item

value.name: Complete
value.notes: Runs when the laptop request form is completed.
value.title: Kinetic Core :: Service Catalog > Laptop Request :: Complete

outputs.name: Adjusted Cost
outputs.description: The total adjusted cost of the item

value.name: Complete
value.notes: Runs when the laptop request form is completed.
value.title: Kinetic Core :: Service Catalog > Laptop Request :: Complete

AccessKeyId.name: id
AccessKeyId.description: The id of the access key

AccessKeyInclude.name: include
AccessKeyInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

CategoryInclude.name: include
CategoryInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

CategoryPolicyRuleName.name: policyRuleName
CategoryPolicyRuleName.description: The name of the policy rule

EngineInclude.name: include
EngineInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

ErrorCount.name: count
ErrorCount.description: Optional parameter to return only the count of the number of task errors that match the criteria.

ErrorEnd.name: end
ErrorEnd.description:
UTC formatted timestamp to use as the ending date.

This value is excluded in the results (<).

* Format: `yyyy-MM-dd`

    Example: 2017-07-27

* Format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ`

    Example: 2017-07-27T15:00:00Z

ErrorGroupFragment.name: groupFragment
ErrorGroupFragment.description: Optional source group (matches any part of the source group: 'like' match).

ErrorHandlerId.name: handlerId
ErrorHandlerId.description:
Optional definition id of the handler.

This value cannot be used if the `nodeId` parameter is also specified.

ErrorIdQuery.name: id
ErrorIdQuery.description:
The id of the errors.

If this parameter is used, all others are ignored.

ErrorInclude.name: include
ErrorInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

ErrorLimit.name: limit
ErrorLimit.description: Number of results to limit the search to. Ignored if the `count` parameter is also provided.

ErrorNodeId.name: nodeId
ErrorNodeId.description: Optional id of the tree node.

ErrorOffset.name: offset
ErrorOffset.description: Offset beginning for paginated results. Ignored if the `count` parameter is also provided.

ErrorRelatedItem1Id.name: relatedItem1Id
ErrorRelatedItem1Id.description: This value is for more advanced usage.

ErrorRelatedItem1Type.name: relatedItem1Type
ErrorRelatedItem1Type.description: This value is for more advanced usage.

ErrorRelatedItem2Id.name: relatedItem2Id
ErrorRelatedItem2Id.description:
This value is for more advanced usage.

Setting either the `nodeId` or `handlerId` parameters will automatically use the correct value.

ErrorRelatedItem2Type.name: relatedItem2Type
ErrorRelatedItem2Type.description:
This value is for more advanced usage.

Setting either the `nodeId` or `handlerId` parameters will automatically use the correct value.

ErrorSource.name: source
ErrorSource.description: Optional name of the source the errors belong to.

ErrorStart.name: start
ErrorStart.description:
UTC formatted timestamp to use as the starting date.

This value is inclusive in the results (>=).

* Format: `yyyy-MM-dd`

    Example: 2017-07-27

* Format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ`

    Example: 2017-07-27T15:00:00Z

ErrorStatus.name: status
ErrorStatus.description:
Optional status of the errors.

If no value is provided, errors of both states may be returned.

ErrorTimeline.name: timeline
ErrorTimeline.description: Field to use as the timeline when searching by dates.

ErrorTreeFragment.name: treeFragment
ErrorTreeFragment.description: Optional tree name (matches any part of the source group: 'like' match).

GroupLoginId.name: loginId
GroupLoginId.description: The loginId of the user to remove from the group.

HandlerForce.name: force
HandlerForce.description: when true, indicates an existing task handler should be overwritten with the package being uploaded.

schema.description: Simulates notices that may not already be present

PolicyRuleDirection.name: direction
PolicyRuleDirection.description: Direction to order the results, ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC). Must also include the *orderBy* parameter when using this parameter.

PolicyRuleLimit.name: limit
PolicyRuleLimit.description: Limit the number of results to the specified value.

PolicyRuleName.name: name
PolicyRuleName.description: The name of the policy rule

PolicyRuleOffset.name: offset
PolicyRuleOffset.description: The row number of the first record to retrieve.

PolicyRuleOrderBy.name: orderBy
PolicyRuleOrderBy.description: Name of the field to order the results by. By default, the results are sorted ascending by the following combination of fields - `type`.

RunCount.name: count
RunCount.description:
Optional parameter.
When not present, both the records and count that match the criteria will be returned.
When present and set to "true", suppress the records and only return the count that matches the criteria.
When present and set to "false", suppress the count and return the records that match the query along with the *more* property to indicate if more records match the criteria.

RunDirection.name: orderBy
RunDirection.description: Direction to order the results, ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC). Must also include the *orderBy* parameter when using this parameter.

RunEnd.name: end
RunEnd.description:
UTC formatted timestamp to use as the ending date for the `createdAt` field. This value is excluded in the results (<).

    Format: `yyyy-MM-dd`
        Example: 2017-07-27

    Format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ`
        Example: 2017-07-27T15:00:00Z

RunGroupFragment.name: groupFragment
RunGroupFragment.description: Optional source group (matches any part of the source group - like match)

RunIdQuery.name: id
RunIdQuery.description: Optional Id of the run to search for. If this parameter is used, all others are ignored.

RunAfterId.name: afterId
RunAfterId.description: Optional, search for runs that have an Id greater than this value.

RunBeforeId.name: beforeId
RunBeforeId.description: Optional, search for runs that have an Id less than this value.

RunIncludeSystem.name: includeSystem
RunIncludeSystem.description: Optional whether to include system runs (Source: Kinetic Task). Ignored if the *source* parameter is also provided.

RunLimit.name: limit
RunLimit.description: Number of results to limit the search to. Ignored if the `count` parameter is also provided.

RunOffset.name: offset
RunOffset.description: Offset beginning for paginated results. Ignored if the `count` parameter is also provided.

RunOrderBy.name: orderBy
RunOrderBy.description: Name of the field to order the results by. By default, the results are sorted descending by the `id` field.

RunOriginatingId.name: originatingId
RunOriginatingId.description: Optional Id of the originating run.

RunParentId.name: parentId
RunParentId.description: Optional Id of the parent run.

RunSource.name: source
RunSource.description: Optional name of the source the runs belong to.

RunSourceGroupRequired.name: sourceGroup
RunSourceGroupRequired.description: Name of the source group the tree belongs to

RunSourceNameRequired.name: sourceName
RunSourceNameRequired.description: Name of the source the tree belongs to

RunStart.name: start
RunStart.description:
UTC formatted timestamp to use as the starting date for the `createdAt` field. This value is inclusive in the results (>=).

    Format: `yyyy-MM-dd`
        Example: 2017-07-27

    Format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ`
        Example: 2017-07-27T15:00:00Z

RunTaskToken.name: token
RunTaskToken.description: The token that identifies the deferred task.

RunTreeFragment.name: treeFragment
RunTreeFragment.description: Optional tree name (matches any part of the tree name - like match)

RunTreeType.name: treeType
RunTreeType.description: Optional runs that belong to the specified type of tree / routine.

Simulate.name: simulate
Simulate.description: when true, simulates doing the work without actually writing anything

SourceInclude.name: include
SourceInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

SourceLimit.name: limit
SourceLimit.description: Number of results to limit the search to.

SourcePolicyRuleName.name: policyRuleName
SourcePolicyRuleName.description: The name of the policy rule

SystemErrorInclude.name: include
SystemErrorInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

TreeDefinitionId.name: definitionId
TreeDefinitionId.description: The definitionId of the routine to show usage.

TreeDirection.name: direction
TreeDirection.description: Direction to order the results, ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC). Must also include the *orderBy* parameter when using this parameter.

TreeForce.name: force
TreeForce.description: when true, indicates an existing tree should be overwritten with the content being uploaded.

TreeGroup.name: group
TreeGroup.description: Optional group the trees belong to (exact match)

TreeGroupArray.name: group[]
TreeGroupArray.description: Optional combination of groups the trees belong to (exact match)

TreeGroupFragment.name: groupFragment
TreeGroupFragment.description: Optional group the trees belong to (matches any part of the source group - like match)

TreeInclude.name: include
TreeInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

TreeLimit.name: limit
TreeLimit.description: Limit the number of results to the specified value.

TreeName.name: name
TreeName.description: Optional name of the trees (exact match)

TreeNameFragment.name: nameFragment
TreeNameFragment.description: Optional name of the trees (matches any part of the tree name - like match)

TreeOffset.name: offset
TreeOffset.description: The row number of the first record to retrieve.

TreeOrderBy.name: orderBy
TreeOrderBy.description: Name of the field to order the results by. By default, the results are sorted ascending by the following combination of fields - `Source Name`, `Source Group`, `Name`.

TreeOwnerEmail.name: ownerEmail
TreeOwnerEmail.description: Optional the process owner's email address.

TreeSource.name: source
TreeSource.description: Optional name of the source the trees belong to.

TreeTitle.name: title
TreeTitle.description: The title of the tree, which includes the Source name, the Source group, and the Tree name.

TreeType.name: type
TreeType.description: Optional type of tree / routine to retrieve.

TriggerAction.name: action
TriggerAction.description: Optional Action describing the type of trigger.

TriggerEnd.name: end
TriggerEnd.description: ISO8601 (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z') formatted date/time where the timeline field is earlier than this value.

TriggerInclude.name: include
TriggerInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

TriggerLimit.name: limit
TriggerLimit.description: Number of results to limit the search to.

TriggerManagementAction.name: managementAction
TriggerManagementAction.description: Optional action taken for managed triggers.

TriggerSelectionCriterion.name: selectionCriterion
TriggerSelectionCriterion.description: Optional selection criterion that matches the selection criterion of a handler.

TriggerSource.name: source
TriggerSource.description: Optional name of the source the triggers belong to.

TriggerSourceGroup.name: group
TriggerSourceGroup.description: Optional source group - only used if source is also provided.

TriggerSourceId.name: sourceId
TriggerSourceId.description: Optional sourceId - only used if source is also provided.

TriggerStart.name: start
TriggerStart.description: ISO8601 (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z') formatted date/time where the timeline field is later than this value.

TriggerTimeline.name: timeline
TriggerTimeline.description: date/time field to use for sorting

TriggerToken.name: token
TriggerToken.description: Optional unique value of the deferred task to resume.

TriggerTree.name: tree
TriggerTree.description: Optional name of the tree - only used if source is also provided.

TriggerTreeType.name: treeType
TriggerTreeType.description: Optional triggers that belong to the specified type of tree / routine.

UserInclude.name: include
UserInclude.description: comma-separated list of additional properties to include in the response

UserLimit.name: limit
UserLimit.description: Number of users to limit the results to

UserOffset.name: offset
UserOffset.description: Record offset to start the page of results with

400.description:
Bad Request


The request was made with an invalid parameter or body content.  The request should not be submitted again with the same values.  This error indicates the user provided invalid content, and the request may be made again after the user changes either the parameters and/or body content.

401.description:
Unauthorized


The request requires the user to be authenticated before receiving a successful response.  The user should either authenticate using a login form, or by sending in the authentication credentials token using the HTTP Basic Authentication header (Authorization).

403.description:
Forbidden


The authenticated user performing the request does not have access to the resource.  Try again with a different user account.

404.description:
Not Found


The resource for the provided URL does not exist.  The URL should be checked to validate all path parameters are using the correct value.

500.description:
Server Error


There was an internal server error while processing the request.  This is likely a bug in the application and should be reported to a system administrator.

title.example: Source Name :: Group Name :: Tree Name

count.description: count of all records that match the query, will not be present when the *count=false* parameter is provided

more.description: indicates more records exist then what was returned, will not be present unless the *count=false* parameter is provided

identifier.description: The unique `name` that identifies the Access Key

secret.description: The secret password that corresponds to this Access Key

items.description: name of the 'Category Access' policy rule

message.example: The task is still in progress, now at 82% complete.
